name: yggdrasil
description: One shared, durable memory for your AI agents — local-first, zero-dependency. Connects Claude Desktop to your running Yggdrasil engine.
long_description: Yggdrasil gives your AI agents a durable, cross-session, cross-tool memory of your own work. This extension exposes the Yggdrasil memory tools (recall / remember / search / bootstrap) to Claude Desktop over stdio. It is a thin facade: it talks to the local Yggdrasil engine you already run (default http://127.0.0.1:42069), so Claude Desktop shares the exact same memory as Claude Code and Codex. Zero Python dependencies — runs on your system python3.

tools.name: ygg_bootstrap
tools.description: Search project-scoped durable memory before non-trivial work.

tools.name: ygg_recall
tools.description: Search durable memory across ALL projects for prior solutions and lessons.

tools.name: ygg_remember
tools.description: Save reusable project memory (refuses common secret patterns).

tools.name: ygg_materialize
tools.description: Export one memory id to an Obsidian-readable Markdown note.

engine_token.title: Engine token
engine_token.description: Bearer token for your local Yggdrasil engine. Get it by running `ygg token` (or: cat ~/.yggdrasil/token) and paste the value here.

engine_url.title: Engine URL
engine_url.description: Where the Yggdrasil engine listens. Leave the default for a standard local install.
